name: Greek entity meliae
shortDescription: The Meliae were nymphs of the ash tree in Greek mythology. They are typically considered to have sprung from the blood of Uranus when Cronus castrated him, making them primordial beings.
description: The Meliae were nymphs associated with the ash tree (Melia) in Greek mythology. They are often considered to be among the most ancient of nymphs, born from the blood of Uranus when Cronus castrated him using an adamantine sickle. As such, they are tied to the very beginnings of the cosmos and the separation of sky and earth. The Meliae were believed to be nurses and protectors of newborn children, especially Zeus in some accounts, and were also associated with honey and manna, suggesting a nurturing and life-giving role. They are sometimes considered the mothers of the Silver Race of humans, a generation that followed the Golden Race. Their connection to ash trees links them to spears and other weapons, as ash wood was commonly used for their creation. While not as prominent as other nymphs like the Naiads or Dryads, the Meliae represent an ancient and powerful connection to the primordial forces of creation and the nurturing aspects of nature.
